journal_entry.rationale: Dividend declared creates an obligation to pay shareholders (liability increase) and reduces retained earnings (equity decrease via dividend account). The debit tuple indicates b=1 (dividend increase), and credit tuple indicates a=1 (liability increase). This represents the declaration stage where the company commits to paying dividends to shareholders, creating a Dividends Declared or Dividends Payable liability. Dividends are not tax-deductible and dividend declarations are not subject to HST/GST.

output.reason: Declaring a dividend creates a liability (dividends payable) to the shareholders. This is a liability increase, so it is credited.

credit_classifier.reason: Declaring a dividend creates a liability (dividends payable) to the shareholders. This is a liability increase, so it is credited.

output.reason: Declaring a dividend increases the Dividend account (owner withdrawal). Under IFRS, the debit side records: 1 dividend increase. This is not an expense but rather a distribution of earnings to owners.

debit_classifier.reason: Declaring a dividend increases the Dividend account (owner withdrawal). Under IFRS, the debit side records: 1 dividend increase. This is not an expense but rather a distribution of earnings to owners.
